raw_consumer_statement: I hope I have categorized this correctly. My Citibank Mastercard was hacked in 2020 and Citibank issued us a new card. I recently received a bill for {$63.00} from XXXX XXXX for a XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. These charges were against the hacked credit card that we no longer have. As I investigated this I discovered that for the past 4 years they have been charging against the hacked credit card that we no longer have. No one has used this XXXX account for at least 7 years. Citibank says merchants charging against an expired credit card is allowed if this is a reoccurring charge. I can not believe that Citibank sends me a new card because my card has been hacked and then proceeded to let a merchant charge against the hacked card. I think Citibank owes me $ XXXX {$250.00}. If Citibank says this is legal and ethical we have a serious problem. I am also a little upset with XXXX for allowing this account to be charged for the previous 7 years when there was no activity on the account. I have cancelled the XXXX account and based on XXXX response I will also be discontinuing my Citibank XXXX. I do not consider them to be ethical after my discussion with them. Is there anything you can do to help. My wife and I are older and this is not only frustrating but to me unethical.
